text: Description: Nuclei is a fast, template-based vulnerability scanner designed to probe applications for security issues. It uses YAML-based templates to define detection logic for various vulnerabilities, enabling efficient and customizable security assessments.
Go version 1.20 or later (if installing from source)
Sufficient disk space for templates (~500MB with all templates)
Download the latest release from GitHub Releases
Move the binary to your PATH: sudo mv nuclei /usr/local/bin/
After installing Nuclei, update the templates:
Custom templates: Create in any directory, specify with -t flag
NUCLEI_CACHE_DIR: Directory to store cached data
Not applicable - Nuclei doesn't require authentication to run.
Support for multiple protocols (HTTP, DNS, TCP, etc.)
Rate limiting and automatic request throttling
-u, -target: Target URL, IP, or CIDR range
-t, -templates: Templates or template directories to use
-severity: Filter by template severity (info, low, medium, high, critical)
-rate-limit: Maximum number of requests per second
-timeout: Timeout in seconds for each template execution
Scans a single URL using the default template set. Useful for quick reconnaissance and initial vulnerability discovery. Best used as a baseline scan before narrowing scope.
nuclei -list urls.txt  # File containing targets like testphp.vulnweb.com, juice-shop.herokuapp.com
Reads multiple targets from a file and scans them sequentially. Ideal for bug bounty programs and enterprise-wide assessments. Ensures consistent testing across a large asset list.
Automatically selects relevant templates based on detected technologies. Reduces noise and unnecessary requests. Recommended for safe, fast reconnaissance scans.
Runs the complete template library against the target. Provides maximum coverage but increases scan time and noise. Should be rate-limited for public or production systems.
Executes a single CVE template. Used for targeted validation of known vulnerabilities.  Ideal during patch verification or incident response.
# Scan with multiple templates - good for testphp.vulnweb.com which has many vulnerabilities
Combines CVE detection with generic vulnerability templates. Useful for intentionally vulnerable environments like VulnWeb.Balances coverage and performance.
Excludes aggressive or noisy templates. Prevents accidental denial-of-service conditions. Recommended for scoped or sensitive environments.
# Scan with templates by severity - testphp.vulnweb.com has many high severity issues
Runs only high-impact vulnerability checks. Efficient for time-limited engagements.  Prioritizes findings with the greatest business risk.
# Scan with templates by tags - testphp.vulnweb.com has SQL injection vulnerabilities
Executes only templates matching specified vulnerability classes. Useful for focused testing based on threat models. Reduces scan duration and false positives.
Executes a chained workflow tailored for WordPress targets. Automatically detects technology before running relevant exploits. Provides structured, intelligent scanning.
Extends workflow logic with additional vulnerability templates. Enhances detection coverage beyond default workflow steps. Useful for deeper CMS assessments.
# Multiple workflows - works well on sites with multiple technologies
Runs multiple workflows sequentially. Ideal for complex targets using multiple technologies. Ensures layered detection without manual intervention.
Detects misconfigurations, injections, and logic flaws. Forms the core of most web application security tests. Best combined with manual validation.
# Specifically scan for XSS vulnerabilities - testphp.vulnweb.com has XSS in search functionality
Focuses specifically on cross-site scripting detection. Verbose mode shows payloads and responses. Useful for identifying reflected and stored XSS vectors.
# Scan for Local File Inclusion - scanme.sh has LFI vulnerabilities
Tests for unauthorized file access via path traversal. Detects both Linux and Windows file disclosure patterns. Critical for backend data exposure risks.
Checks for command injection and RCE conditions. High-risk vulnerability class with severe impact. Findings must be manually validated.
# Scan for SSRF vulnerabilities - public SSRF lab
Detects server-side request forgery vulnerabilities. Useful against cloud and internal network exposures. Often chained with metadata service exploitation.
Runs all known CVE templates against the target. Provides wide historical vulnerability coverage. Should be scoped carefully to avoid excessive requests.
# Scan for specific year CVEs - good for older frameworks
Limits scanning to vulnerabilities disclosed in a specific year. Useful for legacy systems. Reduces scan overhead.
# Scan for specific CVE - Log4j on public test instance
Checks for Log4j JNDI injection vulnerability. Critical RCE vulnerability affecting Java ecosystems. Often used for compliance verification.
# Scan for Spring4Shell vulnerability on test instance
Tests Spring Framework RCE conditions. Requires precise environment validation. False positives are possible without manual checks.
# Prioritize recent CVEs on public test site
Runs only recently added high-severity templates. Ideal for continuous security monitoring. Ensures coverage of emerging threats.
Identifies frameworks, servers, and CMS platforms. Used for attack surface mapping. Often precedes targeted exploitation.
# Detect specific technologies - WordPress detection on WP test site
Confirms WordPress presence and version hints. Triggers WordPress-specific attack workflows. Useful during CMS audits.
# Detect Joomla on public test site
Identifies Joomla installations. Allows targeted CVE scanning. Reduces unnecessary template execution.
# Scan with request header authentication - JWT test site
Adds Authorization header to all requests. Used for authenticated API testing. Essential for testing protected endpoints.
# Scan with cookie authentication - demo cookie-based auth site
Maintains authenticated session using cookies. Allows vulnerability detection behind login. Common in web app assessments.
# Control number of templates executed in parallel
Limits parallel template execution. Prevents overwhelming target servers. Improves stability.
# Define rate limit for HTTP requests - important for public test sites
Caps HTTP requests per second. Reduces detection risk and WAF blocking. Critical for OPSEC.
# Define max number of HTTP requests per template - reduces load on test sites
Limits number of requests per template. Minimizes backend load. Useful for unstable targets.
# Control scanning timeout - useful for slow-responding test sites
Sets request execution timeout. Avoids hanging scans. Improves scan efficiency.
# Add custom headers to all requests - useful for user-agent detection tests
nuclei -target http://testphp.vulnweb.com -templates ~/.local/nuclei-templates/ -header "User-Agent: Mozilla/5.0 (Windows NT 10.0; Win64; x64) AppleWebKit/537.36"
Masks Nuclei’s default fingerprint. Helps evade basic detection mechanisms. Important for OPSEC.
# Follow redirects - test site with redirection
Follows HTTP 3xx redirects automatically. Ensures accurate vulnerability detection. Useful for modern web apps.
# Follow host redirects - domain with multiple redirect chains
Allows redirection across hostnames. Useful for CDN and SSO-heavy environments. Expands scan coverage.
Target Selection: Start with a well-defined scope
Template Selection: Use appropriate templates for your target
Rate Limiting: Control scan speed to avoid overwhelming targets
Output Management: Save and organize results for analysis
Validation: Always validate findings to remove false positives
Updates: Keep nuclei and templates updated for the latest vulnerability checks
Defining accurate matchers for vulnerability detection
description: Detects a custom vulnerability in target application
For testing purposes, use these publicly accessible vulnerable applications:
Critical and high severity findings first
Always validate findings manually to confirm actual vulnerabilities and avoid false positives.
Error: Error parsing template file: yaml: line X: ... Solution:
Check the template syntax in the mentioned file
Verify template format against the Nuclei template guide
Error: could not connect to host Solution:
Verify target accessibility with curl or ping
Try with increased timeout: nuclei -u https://example.com -timeout 10
Error: Too many requests, rate limiting enabled Solution:
Use -rl-per-host to set per-host rate limits
Check template execution details: nuclei -u https://example.com -debug-req
Scanner activity is easily identifiable in target logs
Traffic patterns may trigger WAF/IDS alerts
Extensive scanning can cause DoS-like effects
Web server logs showing Nuclei's default User-Agent
High volume of similar requests to uncommon endpoints
Traffic spikes with predictable scanning patterns
Use custom User-Agent: nuclei -u https://example.com -header "User-Agent: Mozilla/5.0..."
Target selection to avoid scanning critical infrastructure
Template filtering to use only necessary templates
Burp Suite: Web application security testing
